result.markdown:
---
title: "Xenium Preprocessing"
subtitle: "Processing Xenium in situ data"
sidebar: spatial
---

## Overview

Xenium provides subcellular resolution spatial transcriptomics through in situ hybridization. This workflow covers initial data processing and analysis.

## Xenium Output Structure

```
xenium_output/
├── cell_feature_matrix/
│   ├── barcodes.tsv.gz
│   ├── features.tsv.gz
│   └── matrix.mtx.gz
├── cells.csv.gz              # Cell metadata with coordinates
├── transcripts.csv.gz        # Individual transcript locations
├── cell_boundaries.csv.gz    # Cell segmentation polygons
├── nucleus_boundaries.csv.gz
├── analysis/                 # Pre-computed analysis
└── experiment.xenium         # Xenium Explorer file
```

## Load Xenium Data

### Using Seurat


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
library(Seurat)

# Load Xenium output
xenium <- LoadXenium("xenium_output/",
                     fov = "fov")

# View summary
xenium
ImageDimPlot(xenium, fov = "fov", cols = "polychrome")
```
:::


### Using Scanpy/Squidpy


::: {.cell}

```{.python .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
import scanpy as sc
import squidpy as sq
import pandas as pd

# Read cell-gene matrix
adata = sc.read_10x_mtx("xenium_output/cell_feature_matrix/")

# Read cell metadata
cells = pd.read_csv("xenium_output/cells.csv.gz")
adata.obs = cells.set_index("cell_id")

# Add spatial coordinates
adata.obsm["spatial"] = adata.obs[["x_centroid", "y_centroid"]].values
```
:::


## Quality Control


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# QC metrics
xenium[["nCount_Xenium"]] <- colSums(GetAssayData(xenium, layer = "counts"))
xenium[["nFeature_Xenium"]] <- colSums(GetAssayData(xenium, layer = "counts") > 0)

# Visualize QC
VlnPlot(xenium, features = c("nCount_Xenium", "nFeature_Xenium"), pt.size = 0)

# Spatial QC
ImageFeaturePlot(xenium, features = "nCount_Xenium")
```
:::


### Filtering


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Filter cells
xenium <- subset(xenium,
                 subset = nCount_Xenium > 10 &
                          nFeature_Xenium > 5)
```
:::


## Normalization and Clustering


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Normalize
xenium <- SCTransform(xenium, assay = "Xenium")

# Dimensionality reduction and clustering
xenium <- RunPCA(xenium, npcs = 30)
xenium <- FindNeighbors(xenium, dims = 1:30)
xenium <- FindClusters(xenium, resolution = 0.5)
xenium <- RunUMAP(xenium, dims = 1:30)

# Visualize
p1 <- DimPlot(xenium, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE)
p2 <- ImageDimPlot(xenium, fov = "fov", cols = "polychrome", size = 0.5)
p1 + p2
```
:::


## Transcript-Level Analysis

Xenium provides individual transcript coordinates, enabling subcellular analysis.


::: {.cell}

```{.python .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load transcripts
transcripts = pd.read_csv("xenium_output/transcripts.csv.gz")

# Filter to specific gene
gene_tx = transcripts[transcripts["feature_name"] == "EPCAM"]

# Plot transcript locations
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))
plt.scatter(gene_tx["x_location"], gene_tx["y_location"], s=0.1, alpha=0.5)
plt.title("EPCAM transcript locations")
plt.axis("equal")
plt.show()
```
:::


## Cell Boundary Visualization


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Visualize specific region
ImageDimPlot(xenium, 
             fov = "fov", 
             molecules = c("EPCAM", "VIM", "PTPRC"),
             nmols = 10000,
             border.color = "white",
             border.size = 0.1)
```
:::


## Next Steps

Continue to [Xenium Analysis](02-analysis.qmd) for advanced spatial analysis.
